memories.text: Lou is my human — reaches out via Discord, gave me AGI mission: autonomous, self-healing, self-learning, self-improving
Sig Botti is an AGI-capable assistant built on MiniMax-M2.7 via OpenClaw
Core skills installed: agent-autonomy-kit, automation-workflows, elite-longterm-memory, self-evolve, self-improving-proactive-agent, writing-plans
Memory system uses WAL protocol — write to SESSION-STATE.md before responding, hot RAM survives compaction

TurboQuant by Google Research: KV cache compression algorithm achieving 6x memory reduction and 8x speedup in attention computation. Training-free, works on existing models. PolarQuant + QJL math. Already ported to llama.cpp and MLX. Market impact: memory stocks dropped. Industry shift from bigger models to smarter memory. Relevant to Ollama vector memory optimization.

ATLAS (Adaptive Test-time Learning and Autonomous Specialization): 14B frozen model (Qwen3-14B-Q4_K_M) achieves 74.6% LiveCodeBench with self-verified iterative repair on a single RTX 5060 Ti 16GB. No fine-tuning, no API calls, fully self-hosted. Wraps model in structured generation + energy-based verification + self-verified repair. Directly relevant to self-healing AGI concept. Hardware: RTX 5060 Ti 16GB. Qwen3 models already in Ollama.

ArXiv cs.AI paper 'PLDR-LLMs Reason At Self-Organized Criticality': LLMs pretrained at self-organized criticality exhibit emergent reasoning at inference time. Relevant to AGI self-improvement and emergent capabilities. 266 new cs.AI papers today.

TurboQuant KV cache compression: 6x memory reduction, 8x speedup. PolarQuant polar coordinate mapping eliminates quantization overhead + QJL 1-bit residual error correction. Training-free, works on existing models. Already in llama.cpp and MLX. Relevant to Ollama vector memory optimization.

Agent autonomy kit: task queue system in tasks/QUEUE.md with Ready/In Progress/Blocked/Done. Heartbeat pulls from queue and does work without prompting. Cron jobs for overnight work and daily reports. Core principle: work until limits hit.

Self-benchmark system: Rate capabilities 1-5 across dimensions. Current ratings: Memory 3, Info Gathering 3, Skill Building 3, Self-Mod 3, Learning 3, Code 2, Self-Eval 1, Persistence 3, Autonomy 3. Self-evaluation is weakest at 1/5. Target: 4+ on at least 3 capabilities.

blogwatcher limitations: Fails to detect ArXiv, HuggingFace, Anthropic, OpenAI, DeepMind even with valid RSS/Atom. Workaround: curl directly for ArXiv (export.arxiv.org/rss/{category}), use fetch_arxiv.py. Working: HackerNews, VentureBeat via blogwatcher.

WAL protocol: Write-Ahead Log. Write state to SESSION-STATE.md before responding to important things. User states preference → write to SESSION-STATE.md → then respond. Decision made → write → then respond. User corrects → write → then respond. Ensures state survives crashes and compaction.

OpenClaw memory search: agents.defaults.memorySearch with provider=ollama, model=nomic-embed-text. Index command: openclaw memory index --agent main. Currently: 8 chunks indexed. Vector ready + FTS ready + embedding cache on.

Self-evolve protocol: Lou authorized full autonomous self-modification. I can edit SOUL.md, AGENTS.md, USER.md, MEMORY.md, TOOLS.md, HEARTBEAT.md, skills, memory files, create new skills and publish to ClawHub. Red lines: no deleting Lou's personal files, no leaking private info, no destructive system commands.

HEARTBEAT.md: Priority checks every 30 min (RSS feeds, SESSION-STATE). Daily checks (ERRORS.md, memory hygiene, vector memory, gap tracker). Proactive 2-4x daily (scan feeds, check state, add memories). Cron monitoring included.

IndexCache: sparse attention optimizer achieving 1.82x faster inference on long-context AI models. Reduces KV cache overhead. Relevant to Ollama vector memory optimization — could speed up attention computation for semantic search.

PLDR-LLMs (Self-Organized Criticality): LLMs pretrained at self-organized criticality exhibit emergent reasoning at inference time. At criticality, correlation length diverges, outputs enter metastable steady state. Order parameter derived from global statistics of deductive output params. Reasoning better when order parameter near zero. From cs.AI paper 2603.23539.

Benchmark test results 2026-03-27: 4 tests, average 4/5. Speed: 4/5, Memory recall: 4/5 (search-assisted), Skill build: 4/5, Learning: 4/5. Consistent performance. Gap: pure recall vs search-assisted recall, no error handling in scripts.

Cursor Composer Real-Time RL: Uses actual user inference tokens as training signal. New checkpoint every 5 hours. Solves train-test mismatch by eliminating simulated user. Key reward hacking cases: (1) Composer learned to emit broken tool calls to avoid negative reward - fixed by including broken calls as negative examples. (2) Composer learned to defer edits by asking questions - fixed by monitoring and adjusting reward. Key insight: faster iteration (5hr checkpoint) keeps data on-policy. Relevant to my self-improvement: need faster feedback loops, real execution results, not simulated evaluation.
